parsed_review.summary: CP00-507 (RP16 governance-core, Risk C, 150 units RP16.P07.M06.S09-RP16.P08.M02.S20) passes read-only review with no findings. The pack is descriptor-only and makes no overclaim: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both set production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, with all 28 validation commands at exit_code 0 (including rp16:governance-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, git diff --check). Implementation is real and consistent across the changed files: service.js adds freezeCp507Result plus createGovernanceCoreCp507P07CloseoutP08FoundationCaseSet/Descriptor (pure frozen-object builders, no I/O or runtime dispatch); registry.js defines CP507_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS/NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (attestation chain inherits promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false from the CP480 base); validators.js defines coverage/descriptor validators and Hermes/Claude/Handoff packet builders; index.js re-exports all 7 required public exports via export *; the contract JSON, validator script, README, and model.test.js all reference CP507 consistently. The validator script deep-equals the contract's p07_closeout_p08_foundation_descriptor, cp507_requirements, and cp507_no_write_attestation against the generated objects, catching drift. Counts are internally consistent (P07=100 + P08=50 = 150; deliverable sum=150; partial micro M06 correctly begins at S09 with 14 rows). Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. CP178+ hardening is in place: forbidden_review_evidence enumerates auth failure, zero-byte output, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output ref, missing inspection basis, claude-final-approval claim, source-mutation claim, and production/enterprise-trust claim; the readiness auth-preflight passed with non-zero (1432-byte) parseable JSON stdout; validation-report confirms no full diff/source in the prompt, summary_only_review_allowed=false, and forbidden_tools_present=false. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor-evidence/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs) are correctly excluded by changed-file-scope.json. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are absent as expected — they are produced in later steps of the hardened_review_sequence after this read-only run. This review does not constitute final approval; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim.
